acceptanceCriteria: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins on the Home page at http://localhost/washtabui/home in guest state.
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL in this workflow and must be opened through the Home page person/profile button.
After successful authentication, the application returns the user to the Home page in authenticated state.
validationExpectations: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins on the Home page at http://localhost/washtabui/home in guest state.
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL in this workflow and must be opened through the Home page person/profile button.
After successful authentication, the application returns the user to the Home page in authenticated state.

keywordNames: Wait For Element To Be Ready

approvedScenarios.title: Open Login page from Home page guest state using person/profile button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions from the Home page guest state to the Login page and the Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible and interactive.

approvedScenarios.title: Validate Login page controls visibility and interaction state after opening from Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Username and Password fields accept focus and input interaction, and the Login, Back, and Home buttons are visible and enabled on the Login page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify password input is masked during typing
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Password field masks the entered password characters during input and the plain text password value is not visibly displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Login successfully with approved credentials and return to authenticated Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application authenticates the user and returns to the Home page in authenticated state with authenticated user identity display and authenticated-only feature area visible.

approvedScenarios.title: Reject login with invalid username and invalid password
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, the user remains unauthenticated, and the application does not transition to authenticated Home state.

approvedScenarios.title: Reject login with blank username and blank password
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails and only the generic Failed message is displayed without separate required-field validation messaging, while the user remains unauthenticated on the Login page or equivalent unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Reject login with populated username and blank password
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails and only the generic Failed message is displayed without separate required-field validation messaging, while the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Reject login with blank username and populated password
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails and only the generic Failed message is displayed without separate required-field validation messaging, while the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify all failed authentication attempts display the same generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Each failed authentication attempt displays the same generic Failed message and no alternate or detailed validation messages are shown.

approvedScenarios.title: Return to Home page in unauthenticated state using Back button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page without authenticating the user and authenticated-only UI is not visible.

approvedScenarios.title: Return to Home page in unauthenticated state using Home button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page without authenticating the user and authenticated-only UI is not visible.

approvedScenarios.title: Validate login behavior with leading whitespace in username
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application handles the credential values according to implemented whitespace business behavior and the resulting authentication state and any Failed message are observable and consistent.

approvedScenarios.title: Validate login behavior with trailing whitespace in username
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application handles the credential values according to implemented whitespace business behavior and the resulting authentication state and any Failed message are observable and consistent.

approvedScenarios.title: Validate login behavior with leading whitespace in password
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application handles the credential values according to implemented whitespace business behavior and the resulting authentication state and any Failed message are observable and consistent.

approvedScenarios.title: Validate login behavior with trailing whitespace in password
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application handles the credential values according to implemented whitespace business behavior and the resulting authentication state and any Failed message are observable and consistent.

approvedScenarios.title: Validate whitespace-only submission in Username and Password fields
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails with the generic Failed message only, the user remains unauthenticated, and no separate required-field validation message is displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid password with valid username does not authenticate user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, and the application remains in unauthenticated state without showing authenticated Home UI.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid username with valid password does not authenticate user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, and the user remains unauthenticated.

validations: The application transitions from the Home page guest state to the Login page and the Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible and interactive.
The Username and Password fields accept focus and input interaction, and the Login, Back, and Home buttons are visible and enabled on the Login page.
The Password field masks the entered password characters during input and the plain text password value is not visibly displayed.
The application authenticates the user and returns to the Home page in authenticated state with authenticated user identity display and authenticated-only feature area visible.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, the user remains unauthenticated, and the application does not transition to authenticated Home state.

Authentication fails and only the generic Failed message is displayed without separate required-field validation messaging, while the user remains unauthenticated on the Login page or equivalent unauthenticated state.
Authentication fails and only the generic Failed message is displayed without separate required-field validation messaging, while the user remains unauthenticated.
Each failed authentication attempt displays the same generic Failed message and no alternate or detailed validation messages are shown.
The application returns to the Home page without authenticating the user and authenticated-only UI is not visible.
The application handles the credential values according to implemented whitespace business behavior and the resulting authentication state and any Failed message are observable and consistent.
Authentication fails with the generic Failed message only, the user remains unauthenticated, and no separate required-field validation message is displayed.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, and the application remains in unauthenticated state without showing authenticated Home UI.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, and the user remains unauthenticated.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.

unresolvedGaps: Two separate password elements represent the same user-facing password control using different DOM layers, creating ambiguity for downstream resource and keyword generation.
The element name is implementation-oriented and inconsistent with workflow terminology.
The element name is overly generic and does not clearly indicate business purpose.
The locator uses autogenerated Ionic id 'ion-input-0', which is likely unstable across sessions or builds.
The locator 'id=ion-input-1' appears autogenerated and brittle.
The locator depends on the tokenized visible text '[BTN_LOGIN]', which may be localization-driven or dynamically rendered.
The background container is decorative layout content and provides no workflow or validation value.
The workflow explicitly requires validation that all failed authentication attempts display a generic Failed message, but no failure message element exists in the artifact.
